title: The Innovation Tax: Generative AI Adoption, Productivity Paradox, and Systemic Risk in the U.S. Banking Sector
study_design_description: Using a novel dataset linking SEC 10-Q filings to Federal Reserve regulatory data for 809 financial institutions over 2018–2025, the study employs two complementary identification strategies: Dynamic Spatial Durbin Models (DSDM) to capture network spillovers and Synthetic Difference-in-Differences (SDID) for causal inference using the November 2022 ChatGPT release as an exogenous shock. The treatment period is defined as 2023Q1, when the "Generative AI" revolution became salient to financial institutions. Standard errors are estimated using the placebo-based bootstrap procedure with B=200 bootstrap replications.
primary_task_description: GenAI adoption is identified through systematic text analysis of SEC filings. For each filing, keyword occurrences are counted and two measures are constructed: GenAI Mentions and a binary treatment indicator.
population_description: The initial sample comprises 809 unique bank holding companies observed quarterly from 2018Q1 to 2025Q4. After requiring non-missing values for key variables and a minimum of four quarterly observations, the estimation sample includes 126 banks with complete data, of which 41 are classified as AI adopters (having at least one quarter with positive GenAI mentions) and 85 serve as control banks (zero GenAI mentions throughout the sample period).

arms.description: Banks that mention GenAI in their SEC filings after 2023Q1; classified as AI adopters having at least one quarter with positive GenAI mentions.
Banks that never mention GenAI throughout the sample period; serve as controls.
Large banks (top quartile of average total assets) that are AI adopters.
Large banks (top quartile of average total assets) that are control banks.
Small banks (bottom three quartiles by average total assets) that are AI adopters.
Small banks (bottom three quartiles by average total assets) that are control banks.

outcomes.outcome_description: Return on Assets: Net income divided by total assets for bank i in quarter t, expressed in percentage points. Winsorized at the 1st and 99th percentiles to mitigate outlier influence.
Return on Equity: Net income divided by total equity for bank i in quarter t, expressed in percentage points. Winsorized at the 1st and 99th percentiles.

notes: This paper combines Dynamic Spatial Durbin Models (DSDM) and Synthetic Difference-in-Differences (SDID) to study AI adoption in banking. The DSDM captures network spillovers and cross-sectional associations but faces endogeneity concerns: the positive β coefficient may reflect causal effects, positive selection of high-productivity banks, or reverse causality. The SDID analysis, which uses the November 2022 ChatGPT release as an exogenous shock, documents a significant "Implementation Tax" with heterogeneous effects by bank size. Smaller banks experience substantially larger ROE declines (517 basis points) compared to larger banks (129 basis points). The paper reports both direct effects and network spillovers, with large banks showing massive spillover coefficients (θ=3.13 for ROE). Treatment measure relies on disclosed AI adoption in SEC filings, which may understate actual implementation. Sample period captures only initial phase of GenAI diffusion; longer-term effects may differ. Network analysis based on asset similarity rather than actual AI vendor relationships. Significance levels indicated by asterisks: ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01, marginal p<0.10.
